description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR refactors the sloppy link parser to use delimiters.

Closes #5959 (again)

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
See #5964 for additional information.

The `sloppy-parser` was renamed to `pandoc-link-parser` for clarity. This is because the main difference is to implement spaces in link URLs, which is supported by pandoc. 

The original PR was reverted due to conflicts with `Autolink` and `Link` parsing. How this PR differs is that instead of returning the open delimiter end positions after parsing, we return `-1` so that the default link parser can also add delimiters. 

This is necessary for two reasons. The first is so that we do not have to completely re-implement link parsing, including bare brackets, `[my link]`, or labeled links, `[my link][my label]`. The other reason is to work around a check in the `Autolink` parser. Otherwise, link text which ends in a valid url containing path elements will not parse the closing bracket correctly. For example, `[Example: www.example.com/my-path]` will not parse as a link because `www.example.com/my-path]` is parsed as an `Autolink`. 

This last instance is because the `Autolink` parser has a [check to ignore brackets](https://github.com/lezer-parser/markdown/blob/63872036b2a41f0bc916f7d60e33e32c6e62b835/src/extension.ts#L219-L222) only if `cx.hasOpenLink` is `true`, which is only the case if the default link parser adds delimiters.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

closed_issues.summary: Markdown badge links are not rendered correctly

description.text:
### Description

First of all, thank you for your work on Zettlr — it’s a great tool for writing in Markdown.

I noticed that badge images display correctly, but **adding a link around the badge breaks the rendering**.
Here’s a minimal example to reproduce the issue:

```markdown
<!-- ✅ Works -->
![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)

<!-- ❌ Doesn't work -->
[![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)](https://pandoc.org/)
```

Expected behavior:
The second example should render as a clickable badge that links to `https://pandoc.org/`.

Actual behavior:
The badge is not rendered correctly when wrapped in a link.

### Reproducing

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Open a Markdown file in Zettlr.
2. Paste the code above.

```markdown
[![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)](https://pandoc.org/)
```
3. View the preview.



### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Ubuntu 22.04

### Additional Information

_No response_
